page: Amazon Web Services m8id.96xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 192 physical cores and 384 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes 22,800 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network connectivity is supported by a 100 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, with stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores placing in the top 10% of tested systems. The DDR5 memory subsystem delivers top-tier read and write bandwidth, though PassMark memory scores sit in the middle 50%. The processor's advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), drive top-tier results in extended instructions, encryption, and ray tracing.

While multi-threaded operations and prompt processing for large language models perform strongly, text generation speeds for LLMs are a notable tradeoff, ranging from average to poor due to the lack of dedicated GPU hardware. However, the combination of high-capacity local NVMe storage and high memory bandwidth makes this instance highly efficient for database operations, as evidenced by top-tier Redis SET operations and PassMark database benchmarks. It is well-suited for large-scale databases, software compilation, static web serving, and high-throughput data processing workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services m8id.96xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 384 vCPUs and 192 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. It is configured with 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory and 22,800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, supported by a 100 Gbps baseline network. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, database operations, and memory bandwidth, driven by advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX. However, LLM text generation remains a tradeoff, performing at average to poor levels due to the absence of a GPU. The instance delivers exceptional multi-threaded performance, as demonstrated by top-tier stress-ng and bzip3 decompression results, while maintaining a balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8id.96xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 384 vCPUs, 1.5 TB of DDR5 RAM, and 22.8 TB of NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core CPU and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8id.96xlarge features 384 vCPUs, 1536 GB DDR5 RAM, and 22.8 TB NVMe SSD storage for high-throughput database and compute workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity x86_64 platform combining massive local NVMe storage with top-tier multi-core processing and high-speed DDR5 memory.
bullet_points: 384 vCPUs and 192 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors
1536.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory with 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
22,800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes
Top-tier multi-core CPU, database, and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Tradeoff in LLM text generation speed due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration
Optimized for large-scale databases, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving
